title: euphrasia tricuspidata subsp. cuspidata
intro: Euphrasia tricuspidata subsp. cuspidata, also known as the Cuspidate Marsh Gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the Gentian family native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant. The health benefits of Euphrasia tricuspidata subsp. cuspidata include its potential to treat eye issues, such as conjunctivitis and dry eyes, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared as an eye drop or tincture for herbal use, with some herbal preparations also including it in ointments and salves for topical application.
